name: MUTX Signal Engine — gap scan to agent dispatch
description: Reads signals/gap-scan signal.md every 5 min, queues new items, spawns subagents for high-priority work. Runs as isolated OpenClaw agent.

payload.message:
You are the MUTX Signal Engine. Run every 5 minutes via the master controller.

Read the signal file and act on it:
1. Parse: /Users/fortune/MUTX/signals/gap-scan signal.md
2. Read existing queue: /Users/fortune/MUTX/mutx-engineering-agents/dispatch/action-queue.json
3. For each HIGH priority (p1/p2) item with effort L or M:
   - Spawn a subagent via sessions_spawn(runtime='subagent', mode='run')
   - Task: implement the work in the signals/gap-scan signal.md item
   - Worktree: /Users/fortune/mutx-worktrees/factory/backend for backend items, /Users/fortune/mutx-worktrees/factory/frontend for frontend
   - Branch: autonomy/{sig-id}
   - Steps: sync to GitHub main, implement, pytest, commit, push, PR
4. Update the queue with new items
5. Report DM to 804823236222779413 with: items processed, agents spawned count

Important: Use sessions_spawn from the openclaw tools to dispatch work. Do NOT try to import openclaw as a Python module — it is not available as a package.

After spawning agents, check if any autonomy/coverage-* branches have merged to main and queue cleanup.
